entries.note: The LiveStreamPane component, all four block renderers (TextBlockRenderer, ThinkingBlockRenderer, ToolCallRenderer, SystemNoticeRenderer), the rAF-batched renderStore, and the normalized render-model reducer were already fully implemented in a prior session. This attempt added the missing e2e test coverage. Six stream frame builder functions were added to helpers.ts (sessionStartedFrame, sessionEndedFrame, streamTextFrame, streamThinkingFrame, streamToolUseFrame, streamToolResultFrame, streamSystemNoticeFrame) so all stream-pane tests can construct realistic WS message sequences. A data-testid attribute was added to the scroll container in LiveStreamPane so the upscroll tests can reliably target that element. stream-pane.spec.ts covers: TextBlock rendering (plain and fenced code), ThinkingBlock collapsed-by-default and expand-on-click (AC-6), ToolCallRenderer name/badge/expand/result-update (AC-7), SystemNotice info and warn severity, session.started and session.ended lifecycle notices, follow-tail real-time visibility (AC-3), upscroll detach showing the Jump to latest pill (AC-4), and Jump to latest click re-engaging follow-tail (AC-5). AC-1 (jank-free 1k+ blocks) requires Chrome DevTools profiling and is not automatable in Playwright. AC-2 (variable-height blocks measure without overlap) is tested implicitly through all block-rendering assertions but cannot be verified via DOM assertions alone. AC-8 (Load earlier messages — truncated sentinel) requires filling the 10k-block ring cap which is not feasible in headless e2e; the code path was reviewed and is implemented. AC-9 (session scroll position preserved on tab switch) deferred because the current WorkflowDetailRoute UI does not expose a session-switch control; the scroll cache is in place and implemented correctly.
deferred_criteria: AC-1: 1k+ block jank-free rendering — requires Chrome DevTools perf profiling, not automatable in Playwright; implementation uses @tanstack/react-virtual with measureElement
AC-2: variable-height blocks measure correctly — implicitly covered by all block-rendering assertions but no precise DOM height assertion is available
AC-8: Load earlier messages button — requires 10,001+ blocks to trigger the truncated sentinel; not feasible in headless e2e; code path exists in LiveStreamPane.loadEarlier()
AC-9: Switching between sessions preserves scroll position — scrollPositionCache is implemented but WorkflowDetailRoute has no session-switch UI to drive from a test
known_risks: The upscroll/Jump-to-latest tests (AC-4, AC-5) rely on the virtualized container overflowing with 30 blocks. If the Playwright viewport is very tall or block heights are much smaller than estimated, the container may not overflow and the scroll dispatch will not detach follow-tail. Blocks use long content strings to ensure measurable height.

rAF batching: text frames sent in rapid succession are flushed together in the next animation frame. If requestAnimationFrame fires before all 30 frames arrive from the WS mock, some may land in a later batch. Playwright's expect().toBeVisible() retries until timeout so this is absorbed on most machines, but very slow CI might see intermittent failures.
The data-testid on the scroll container will conflict if a second LiveStreamPane or ReviewPanel with an overflow-y-auto container is ever rendered alongside it in the same view.
